Notice Of Motion To Secure Costs For Nonresident Pltf Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 1030
Off Calendar Sep-15-2005 Denied
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING.

DEFENDANT ANDREA GOSLINE'S Notice Of Motion To Secure Costs For Nonresident Plaintiff Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 1030 is DENIED. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Counsel for plaintiff left the courtroom before the case was called and did not return.
Judge James L. Warren, Reporter Christina Paxton CSR #1558. (301/JLW)

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-07-2005 Continued To Nov-09-05 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Motion For Leave Of Court For Mental Examination Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Sep-07-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: NOEL HIBBARD, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY
WILLIAM SIMPICH, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY

HEARING HELD. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED.
Defendant's Motion For Leave Of Court For Mental Examination of Plaintiff is denied based upon Plaintiff's stipulation that she is not making a claim for mental and emotional distress over and above that usually associated with the physical injuries claimed and that no expert testimony regarding this usual mental and emotional distress will be presented at trial in support of her claim for damages.
This court will not determine whether the hynotherapist is qualified to offer testimony regarding physical injuries. The expertise of the witness is an issue for the trial court to determine. The argument that the emotional distress claimed by Plaintiff is over and above that usually associated with the physical injuries claimed has no merit.
Late opposition has been considered. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
